item.title: Anthropic now has more business customers than OpenAI, according to Ramp data
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: For the first time, Anthropic has more verified business customers than OpenAI, according to this month’s AI Index from the fintech firm Ramp.

item.title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today’s tougher startup market
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: From October 13-15, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages, led by 250+ tech leaders shaping the industry today. Register now to save up to $410, plus 50% off a second pass.

item.title: Poppy debuts a proactive AI assistant to help organize your digital life
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Poppy is an AI-powered app that connects your calendar, email, messages, and other services to surface reminders, suggestions, and tasks based on what’s happening in your life.

item.title: Adaption aims big with AutoScientist, an AI tool that helps models train themselves
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Adaption's new AutoScientist tool is designed to let models adapt to specific capabilities quickly through an automated approach to conventional fine-tuning.

item.title: Medicare’s new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: There is no governmental mechanism to pay for an AI agent that monitors a patient between visits, calls to check in, coordinates a housing referral, or makes sure someone picks up their medication. ACCESS creates that mechanism for the first time.

item.title: Kevin Hartz’s A* just closed its third fund with $450M
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: The firm takes a generalist approach, backing companies across categories such as AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security. The average check size for this fund will be between $3 million and $5 million, with the aim to back at least 30 startups.

item.title: Former Tesla exec and Heron Power CEO Drew Baglino has founded a heat pump startup
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Sadi Thermal Machines is Drew Baglino's second startup since leaving Tesla in 2024.

item.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his experience running the prominent startup accelerator Y Combinator, knew "founders who had control usually did not give it up."

item.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: "Any sale or transfer of Anthropic stock, or any interest in Anthropic stock, offered by these firms is void and will not be recognized on our books and records," the company's support page reads.

item.title: Potholes cost cities millions: This company is using AI and trucks to fix them
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Fleet management company Samsara has developed an AI model to detect different kinds of potholes and gauge how fast they're deteriorating.

item.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google and SpaceX are in talks to build data centers in orbit, pitching space as the future home for AI compute, even as costs today remain far higher than on the ground.

item.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez, Ivan Mehta, Aisha Malik
item.description: Google unveiled its new AI-first Googlebooks laptops, more agentic Gemini features, vibe-coded Android widgets, Gemini in Chrome, refreshed Android Auto, and more ahead of I/O.

item.title: The AI legal services industry is heating up — Anthropic is getting in on the action
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic's new tools are designed to help law firms automate specific clerical functions — things like document search and review, case law resources, deposition prep, document drafting, and other related areas.

item.title: Google adds Gemini-powered dictation to Gboard, which could be bad news for dictation startups
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Google's transcription feature will initially launch with Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel phones.

item.title: Android adds a feature to stop you from doomscrolling
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google’s new Android feature, Pause Point, forces you to wait before opening distracting apps in an effort to curb addictive scrolling habits.

item.title: Google unveils Googlebook, a new line of AI-native laptops
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The company says Googlebooks, which are launching this fall, are the first laptops designed from the ground up for Gemini Intelligence to offer personal and proactive help.

item.title: Google’s ‘Create My Widget’ feature will let you vibe-code your own widgets
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: To create a widget, users will be able to describe what they want using natural language. For example, you could ask the feature to "suggest three high-protein meal prep recipes every week" in order to get a custom dashboard that you can add and resize on your home screen.

item.title: Google brings agentic AI and vibe-coded widgets to Android
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Gemini Intelligence will also include Gboard-based dictation and form-filling capabilities.

item.title: Google launches new Android security feature to help uncover spyware attacks
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Intrusion Logging is a new part of Android’s Advanced Protection Mode, which aims to help protect human rights activists, journalists, and dissidents from government spyware attack and law enforcement forensic devices.

item.title: Indigo brings the open social web to one app
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Indigo's new social app lets you cross-post to the open social web, including Mastodon and Bluesky, and browse a unified timeline.
